findings.description: subprocess.run with shell=True over an f-string built from untrusted job.payload values (source/dest) is a textbook OS command injection — a job with dest='x; rm -rf /' executes verbatim.
attempt is reset to 0 every loop iteration, so the attempt<MAX_RETRIES guard never advances and execute_job returning False forever yields an infinite loop instead of 3 retries.
backoff RETRY_BACKOFF_SECONDS*(attempt+1) always evaluates to 2.0 because attempt was just zeroed, so the intended exponential/increasing backoff never happens.
blocking time.sleep inside an async def poll_loop freezes the entire event loop for the full poll interval, defeating the asyncio design and starving every other coroutine.
run_with_retries is fully synchronous and never awaited, so poll_loop being declared async is misleading and provides no concurrency.
while True with the default stop_after=None has no break or cancellation path, making the loop unstoppable unless a caller remembers to pass stop_after.
list.pop(0) is O(n) per dequeue, turning queue drain into accidentally-quadratic behavior; collections.deque would be O(1).
subprocess.run lacks a timeout, so a hung convert process blocks the synchronous worker indefinitely and wedges the whole queue.
sum(values) trusts untrusted payload data with no type/shape validation, raising TypeError on non-numeric input that is then silently swallowed and retried to the same failure.
blanket except Exception swallows all errors including programming bugs, retries non-transient failures pointlessly, and never distinguishes retry-worthy from permanent failures.
source/dest default to empty string so a malformed convert job runs 'convert  ' silently instead of failing fast on missing required fields.
inconsistent typing style mixes typing.Optional with PEP 585 builtin generics (dict[str, Any]); pick one convention.
